Hearing re: PLAINTIFFS JANICE COOK, RANDY COOK'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A) And 36(D).

The matter is contested. Argument/discussions are held on the portion of the tenative ruling requiring the filing of motions for summary judgment/adjudication on regular notice time. Argument/discussions conclude. The parties stipulate that summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on 60 days' notice.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification as to the 60 days' notice for motions for summary judgment/adjudication as follows:

The Court exercises its discretion and GRANTS Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff under C.C.P. Section 36 (a) and (d). Plaintiffs provided clear and convincing medical documentation from Dr. Kjelland that Ms. Cook suffers from incurable malignant pleural mesothelioma, raising substantial medical doubt of her survival beyond six months. The Court finds that Ms. Cook's health is such that the interests of justice will be served by granting this preference. Opposition filed. Conditional Oppositions filed. Joinder filed. Reply filed.

1. The trial date is April 28, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is May 7, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 22, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on 60 days' notice.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is April 11, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is April 25, 2025.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 6, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

8. No later than January 21, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 2.)

Hearing re; DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

This matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant United Airlines, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiffs' Complaint is OVERRULED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. As to United's assertion that the 2006 bankruptcy order bars Plaintiffs' claim, the determination is highly factual and not appropriate for demurrer. (See Flores v. Kmart Corp. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1316; see In re Zilog, Inc. (9th Cir.) 450 F.3d 996; see In re Grossman's Inc. (3rd. Cir. 2010) 607 F.3d 114.) Accordingly, pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(d), the Court declines to take judicial notice of the 2006 bankruptcy order because it is irrelevant.

Order signed.
